definition: A journal article, working paper, preprint, book chapter, or report-like scholarly paper.
coding_notes: Use for the citeable work itself, not for a downloaded PDF file when the file is merely an access route.

definition: A trial, preregistration, PAP, protocol, or registration record.
coding_notes: Use for ClinicalTrials.gov, AEA, EGAP, RIDIE, OSF registration, and similar records.

definition: A curated collection that indexes or codes many papers/results.
coding_notes: Use for SCORE, FReD, RPP, Metabus, or meta-research corpora.

definition: A structured source with rows of results or metadata.
coding_notes: Use when the source is a database/table rather than a scholarly corpus paper.

definition: A repository, Dataverse, OSF, Zenodo, OpenICPSR, GitHub, zip, or replication package.
coding_notes: Use for source packages that contain files supporting papers/results.

definition: A standalone PDF file used as a source object.
coding_notes: Prefer paper/report when the PDF represents a citeable work; use pdf for file-level sources.

definition: A policy, technical, institutional, or project report.
coding_notes: Use when the source is not a journal/working paper but is still a citeable report.

definition: A web page used as an extraction or relationship source.
coding_notes: Use only when the page itself is the source object.

definition: A raw or analytic data file.
coding_notes: Use for CSV, TSV, DTA, RDS, SAV, XLSX, or similar data objects.
Use for R, Stata, Python, shell, notebook, or script output sources.

definition: A table produced inside this repository.
coding_notes: Use for internal audit/staging tables; this alone is not original-source evidence.

definition: A source object that does not fit another category.
coding_notes: Use sparingly and explain in source.notes.

definition: A value-bearing source object was mirrored or already exists locally.
coding_notes: Can support level 5 after identity and policy checks.
Supports grounding/resolution, not original-number verification unless metadata contains the value.

definition: Only an abstract or publisher/detail landing page was obtained.
coding_notes: Use as proof of location; promote only if the value appears there.

definition: The route reached a paywall, subscription, or login artifact.
coding_notes: Keep as blocker evidence and do not promote as value-bearing bytes.

definition: The route reached a CAPTCHA or bot-check artifact.
coding_notes: Stop automated retries for this route/host.

definition: The route returned access denied, 403, or equivalent.
coding_notes: Record as blocked unless another lawful route succeeds.

definition: The attempt was blocked by policy, robots, missing permissions, missing key, or similar.
coding_notes: Needs manual review or different route.

definition: No network/file retrieval was attempted or completed.
coding_notes: Use for locator-only rows.
Use for ambiguous candidates or multiple possible targets.

definition: Candidate route was checked and rejected.
coding_notes: Explain mismatch or rejection in decision_reason.
Use for NCT JSON/XML/HTML records and related registry result pulls.
Use when PMID metadata is used to discover DOI/PMCID/full text; this is not full text by itself.
Use for PMC OA XML, BioC, OAI, PDF, or OA package retrieval.
Use for DOI metadata, license, references, and TDM/full-text candidate links.
Use for dataset/repository DOI metadata and file discovery.
Use for OA location/PDF/manuscript candidate discovery.
Use for DOI/PMID/PMCID resolution and OA location discovery.
Use for external IDs, openAccessPdf, and candidate URLs.
Use for life-science metadata, PMCID bridging, and full-text candidate URLs.

definition: A candidate open-access URL discovered by a resolver.
coding_notes: Use for the candidate route; classify the bytes separately with source_byte_class.

definition: A preprint, author manuscript, institutional repository, or repository copy candidate.
coding_notes: Use when the candidate may be a lawful non-VOR source object.

definition: Direct request to DOI redirect or publisher landing page.
coding_notes: Use for final-host page attempts, including paywall/abstract/CAPTCHA outcomes.

definition: A stable URL route not covered by a specialized resolver.
coding_notes: Use for official project pages, direct dataset URLs, or stable archive URLs.

definition: An access route not covered by the controlled list.
coding_notes: Explain the route in access_notes.

definition: Full article PDF bytes for the target work.
coding_notes: Level 5 eligible if identity matches and access/mirroring is lawful.

definition: Full article XML/JATS/NLM-style bytes for the target work.
coding_notes: Level 5 eligible if identity matches and the value could plausibly appear there.

definition: Full article HTML bytes, not just a landing page or abstract.
coding_notes: Level 5 eligible only when actual full text is present.

definition: Author accepted manuscript, preprint, or repository manuscript full text.
coding_notes: Level 5 eligible but flag source_version as manuscript/preprint when available.

definition: Supplementary material used to support extraction.
coding_notes: Level 5 eligible if it could contain the value, table, appendix, or methods.
Level 5 eligible if it can support the value or recomputation.

definition: Script, notebook, log, or code output.
coding_notes: Level 5 eligible if it generates or contains the value.
Level 5 eligible when the registry record is the source for the value or selector.
Level 5 eligible under PMC OA/usage constraints.
Usually level 4/metadata only; level 5 only if the plotted value is actually in the abstract/record.
Use for grounding and resolver provenance; not level 5 for D/N values.

definition: Publisher landing page, title page, or abstract page.
coding_notes: Not level 5 unless the exact plotted value appears on that page.

definition: Paywall, subscription, sign-in, or institutional-access page.
coding_notes: Never promote as value-bearing evidence; keep as access evidence.
Never promote; stop automated retries for the route.
Never promote; record blocker.
Use with extraction_problem/manual to-do.

definition: Bytes were obtained but not classified.
coding_notes: Manual review required before promotion.

definition: No bytes were retrieved for this access row.
coding_notes: Use when the row documents a locator or attempted route only.

definition: Open or public license/access appears to allow local mirroring for this use.
coding_notes: Still preserve license_url and policy notes when available.

definition: Access is public or obtained, but reuse/storage terms are restricted or unclear.
coding_notes: Use when manual/legal review may be needed.

definition: Access appears to require subscription/institutional entitlement.
coding_notes: Do not treat as open mirroring.

definition: License or storage policy has not been determined.
coding_notes: Default when no policy was captured.

definition: License category is not applicable to this access row.
coding_notes: Use for failed/nonretrieved routes or purely internal derived files.
Use for final published article/full text.

definition: Accepted manuscript after peer review but before publisher formatting.
coding_notes: Flag as not VOR.

definition: Author manuscript or institutional repository copy.
coding_notes: Flag as not VOR.
Flag as pre-publication/non-VOR.

definition: Supplementary material associated with another source.
coding_notes: Use for appendices and supporting files.
Use for NCT/AEA/EGAP/RIDIE/OSF registry records.
Use for source data files.
Use for computational source objects.
Not value-bearing unless the value appears in metadata.

definition: Source version has not been determined.
coding_notes: Use until a narrower version can be coded.
Use for VOR/AAM/preprint article files when narrower manuscript/preprint roles are not used.

definition: Author manuscript or accepted manuscript artifact.
coding_notes: Flag version/source separately.
Record preprint version when available.
Use for NCT/AEA/EGAP/RIDIE/OSF registry JSON/XML/pages.
Use for article supplements and supporting packages.
Use for CSV/TSV/DTA/RDS/XLSX/SAV and similar files.

definition: Code, notebook, log, or generated output artifact.
coding_notes: Use for scripts and code execution outputs.
Grounding/resolution only unless values are present in metadata.
Use for manual visual capture of pages/tables/blockers.
Do not promote unless exact value is present.
Use for DOI/publisher/repository landing pages.
Useful for corpus assertions but not original-source evidence.
Inventory extracted members separately when used.
Set parent_file_id to the archive file.
Explain in notes.
Record attempt/access route.

definition: API response stored as an artifact.
coding_notes: Use for registry/metadata JSON/XML.
Examples: OSF, Dataverse, Zenodo, Figshare, openICPSR.
Use only for lawful/allowed access.

definition: File already existed locally before this run.
coding_notes: Preserve checksum and path.
Keep original path/source when known.
Record operator/access notes and rights decision.
Record command in extraction_event.

definition: Produced by this repo's derived-data pipeline.
coding_notes: Not original-source evidence unless backed by support rows.
Link to parent_file_id.
Use until backfilled.

definition: Mirroring/text extraction appears allowed for this project use.
coding_notes: Keep license/terms evidence.

definition: Local/internal use only; do not redistribute bytes.
coding_notes: Expose metadata/snippets only as allowed.
Manual/legal review may be needed.

definition: Source appears to require subscription/institutional entitlement.
coding_notes: Do not treat as public OA.
Use for failed attempts or internal derived artifacts.
Default for pilot backfills.

definition: Rights need human review before promotion/use.
coding_notes: Use for publisher/repository/manual routes with unclear terms.

definition: No external target identity has been established.
coding_notes: Danger level: number cannot be tied to a real source.

definition: An external corpus/database asserts the row, but target identity is weak or incomplete.
coding_notes: Needs source identification before acquisition.

definition: The represented source can be identified bibliographically or by registry/repository ID.
coding_notes: Identity exists but source object not yet located/mirrored.

definition: A DOI, registry, repository, or landing page locates the target.
coding_notes: Located is not mirrored.

definition: A lawful/plausible source object route appears available.
coding_notes: Next action is selective mirroring.

definition: A plausible source object has been mirrored with checksum/path.
coding_notes: Next action is extraction/verification.

definition: Text/data were extracted from a mirrored object.
coding_notes: Next action is value-specific verification.

definition: Value has not been checked against source text/data.
coding_notes: Default for unanchored or unresolved rows.

definition: Value is currently supported only by corpus/database assertion.
coding_notes: Do not count as original-source verification.

definition: A source object was obtained but not yet parsed for the value.
coding_notes: Level 5 but not level 6.

definition: Text/data were extracted but exact D/N support is not confirmed.
coding_notes: Needs value search and support rows.

definition: Relevant text/table/data for the value was found.
coding_notes: Needs parsed transformation check.

definition: D/N or native values were verified from source text/data.
coding_notes: Strict level 6/7 depending on recomputation.

definition: The extraction source directly reports a result about the represented source/work.
coding_notes: Default direct assertion relationship when no narrower type applies.

definition: A corpus or collection includes a paper.
coding_notes: Use for paper membership independent of a specific result value.

definition: A database/registry row reports a result or metadata for a paper/study/trial.
coding_notes: Use when the database is the literal source of copied numbers/text.

definition: A package/repository supplies data, code, outputs, or supplements for a paper.
coding_notes: Use for replication-package support links.

definition: A registry record preregisters or records the study/trial/protocol.
coding_notes: Use for actual registry/PAP artifacts, not vague corpus claims.

definition: A registry record links to a paper/report/results source.
coding_notes: Use for post-trial publication/data/program URLs.

definition: A PAP/MPAP/protocol supports the study/result selector.
coding_notes: Use when a PAP artifact is available outside a registry row.

definition: A registered-report Stage 1/protocol source supports the final paper.
coding_notes: Use for registered-report protocol relationships.

definition: A publication reports results for a registered study.
coding_notes: Use from article to registry when the article itself documents registration.

definition: A source or result is a replication of another source/result.
coding_notes: Use when original and replication sources are known, not only corpus-level assertion.

definition: A source/result computationally reproduces another paper/result.
coding_notes: Use for same-data/code reproduction relationships.

definition: A source/result reanalyzes robustness of another result.
coding_notes: Use for alternative specifications/checks rather than new-sample replication.

definition: A meta-analysis or pooled round includes a component study/result.
coding_notes: Use for pooled/component hierarchies.

definition: A corpus/database asserts that a represented paper/result has specific numbers.
coding_notes: Use when we are taking the corpus row as the immediate evidence source.

definition: A corpus asserts preregistration but no registry/PAP artifact has been obtained.
coding_notes: Keep confidence low unless the artifact is later resolved.

definition: A paper uses or analyzes a dataset/source.
coding_notes: Use for paper-data source relationships.

definition: One source object is derived from another.
coding_notes: Use for extracted tables, converted files, and generated outputs.

definition: Two sources/results represent the same object or claim.
coding_notes: Use for dedupe mapping; do not double-count both.

definition: A parent collection contains a child source/result.
coding_notes: Use for corpus, database, or pooled-round parent-child membership.
Use when a more specific PAP/registered-report/trial subtype is unavailable.
Use for PAP documents and PAP sections.
Use for MPAPs such as Metaketa pooled analysis plans.
Use for accepted protocols before results.
Use when mapping protocol to final registered-report paper.
Use for NCT/AEA/RIDIE/EGAP/OSF-style registry records.
Use for repository/package mappings.
Use for appendices, supplements, and supplemental tables.

definition: Replication intended to repeat the same claim/design closely.
coding_notes: Use for same-claim original/replication pairs.

definition: Replication of a construct using different operationalization/design.
coding_notes: Use when source says conceptual replication or design differs materially.
Use for computational reproducibility checks.
Use for robustness projects.

definition: Replication plus extension or new conditions.
coding_notes: Use when extension is material.

definition: Pilot/smaller study paired with later full-scale/larger study.
coding_notes: Use for pilot-to-main-study pairs.

definition: Component study included in pooled round/meta-analysis.
coding_notes: Use for Metaketa and similar pooled designs.

definition: Paper included as a child of a corpus.
coding_notes: Use for membership without result-level numbers.

definition: Result row included as child of a database/corpus.
coding_notes: Use for row-level source assertions.

definition: Relationship inferred only from citation/link metadata.
coding_notes: Use when no value/selector evidence has been copied.
Use only while staged; add an extraction_problem if it matters for promotion.
Use only when timing evidence supports this.

definition: Relationship/artifact predates analysis/results but data may already exist.
coding_notes: Use for PAPs, protocols, or registered reports when analysis timing is verified.

definition: Relationship/artifact was created after data collection but before results were known or reported.
coding_notes: Use when this distinction is documented.

definition: Relationship/artifact was created after results were known or public.
coding_notes: Do not use as preregistered confirmatory evidence.

definition: Timing is not meaningful for this relationship.
coding_notes: Use for corpus membership, duplicate, and many package-support relationships.

definition: Timing has not been audited or cannot be determined.
coding_notes: Use honestly rather than guessing.

definition: Relationship is supported by copied text.
coding_notes: Preferred when an article/registry/PAP explicitly states the relationship.

definition: Relationship is supported by a structured metadata field.
coding_notes: Use for corpus/database/API columns.

definition: Relationship is supported by DOI matching/linking.
coding_notes: Use when DOI itself is the relationship evidence.

definition: Relationship is supported by registry link/ID.
coding_notes: Use for NCT/AEA/EGAP/RIDIE/OSF registry relationships.

definition: Relationship is supported by citation matching.
coding_notes: Use when title/author/year/citation identifies the target.

definition: Relationship is supported by title/author/year matching.
coding_notes: Use when no durable ID is available.

definition: Relationship is supported by package manifest, file path, or code metadata.
coding_notes: Use for replication packages and package-derived rows.

definition: Relationship currently depends on manual coding judgment.
coding_notes: Use sparingly and keep confidence low/medium.
Use only while staged.

definition: No replication/reproduction relationship is asserted for this result.
coding_notes: Default for ordinary result rows.

definition: This result is the original/target side of a replication pair.
coding_notes: Use when paired to later replication/reproduction.

definition: This result is the replicating/follow-up side.
coding_notes: Use for new-sample/direct/conceptual replication rows.

definition: This result is a computational reproduction of a prior result.
coding_notes: Use for same-data/code reproduction.

definition: This result is a robustness reanalysis of a prior result.
coding_notes: Use for alternative specifications/checks.

definition: The row asserts a replication relationship, but side/source details are incomplete.
coding_notes: Stage until original/replication sides are resolved.
Use only temporarily and add a problem if relevant.

definition: Support object contains or asserts the effect/statistic text.
coding_notes: Use when this object is the literal source of verbatim_effect_text.

definition: Support object contains or asserts the N/sample-size text.
coding_notes: Use when this object is the literal source of verbatim_n_text.

definition: Support object contains or asserts the outcome label/text.
coding_notes: Use when outcome wording comes from a different source than the effect.
Use for registry/PAP/MPAP selector support.

definition: Support object contains inputs needed for D/native conversion.
coding_notes: Use for SDs, control means, event counts, arm Ns, SEs, or formulas.

definition: Support object is raw data for recomputation.
coding_notes: Use for row-level or participant-level data files.
Use when recomputation starts from prepared analysis data.
Use for scripts that produce or transform values.

definition: Support object is generated output from rerunning code.
coding_notes: Use for logs, tables, or saved model outputs.

definition: Support object grounds the identity of the represented work.
coding_notes: Use for DOI/Crossref/PubMed/OpenAlex records and title metadata.

definition: Support object proves route/access status but not the number.
coding_notes: Use for abstracts, landing pages, paywalls, and blocked attempts.

definition: Support object asserts a mapping between sources.
coding_notes: Use for prereg, replication, package, and corpus-membership relationships.
Explain in notes.

definition: Source directly reports standardized mean difference.
coding_notes: Use when the standardization is source-defined.

definition: Log odds ratio converted to d by Chinn formula.
coding_notes: Require log OR and enough context to justify latent-scale conversion.

definition: Arm event counts converted to log OR then d.
coding_notes: Require treatment/control event and non-event counts; record continuity correction if used.
Use d = 2r/sqrt(1-r^2).

definition: Fisher z converted to r then d.
coding_notes: Use r = tanh(z), then r_to_d.

definition: Regression coefficient on a documented standardized outcome.
coding_notes: Use only when outcome standardization source is documented.

definition: A source/corpus provides a D-like proxy but original conversion is not fully verified.
coding_notes: Flag as proxy and avoid claiming original-source conversion.

definition: Paper-level/canonical D is the median of eligible child result Ds.
coding_notes: Preserve child rows and aggregation rule.

definition: No defensible D conversion; native metric only.
coding_notes: Use for native panel rows.

definition: D/native conversion is blocked because inputs are missing.
coding_notes: Add extraction_problem and to-do.
Use only with date/timing evidence.

definition: Preregistration/PAP confirmed before analysis/results but not necessarily before data collection.
coding_notes: Use only with timing evidence.
Use with protocol/stage mapping details.

definition: A proposal/design was locked before fielding but lacks full preregistration/PAP elements.
coding_notes: Useful for TESS-like cases; do not overstate as PAP-confirmed.

definition: A registry record exists but timing/selector/details are not fully audited.
coding_notes: Use until filing date and selector text are verified.

definition: A registry record is identified and sourceable, but its preregistration timing has not been audited.
coding_notes: Use for registry-derived rows until dates/amendments are checked.

definition: A corpus or source label says preregistered, but no registry/PAP artifact URL has been obtained.
coding_notes: Low-confidence prereg evidence; must not become PAP-confirmed without artifact.
Use only when source/corpus explicitly classifies it as not preregistered.

definition: Preregistration status has not been determined.
coding_notes: Use as default outside prereg panels.

definition: Native effect/SE/N are meaningful, but D conversion is not defensible.
coding_notes: Use for clustered/policy/administrative effects lacking D inputs.

definition: Source exists but effect, N, or conversion inputs are missing.

Map duplicate and ensure canonical_result does not double-count it.

definition: A number exists but cannot be tied to an external source or represented work.
coding_notes: Level 0; never public-promote.

definition: The row traces only to this repo's derived/intermediate files.
coding_notes: Level 1; useful for debugging, not public evidence.

definition: An external source asserts the number but does not identify the represented target robustly.
coding_notes: Level 2; corpus assertion without durable child grounding.

definition: An external source asserts the number and provides enough info to find the target source.
coding_notes: Level 3; current minimum floor for public diagnostic plotting.

definition: The represented target source was resolved independently by DOI/PMID/PMCID/NCT/registry/URL/citation database.
coding_notes: Level 4; identity grounded, number not yet verified from original.

definition: Value-bearing source object bytes were obtained or mirrored.
coding_notes: Level 5; metadata/paywall/abstract-only pages do not count unless value is present.

definition: We copied/extracted the exact number/supporting text from original or authoritative source bytes.
coding_notes: Level 6; source-text verification achieved.

definition: We recomputed the number from raw data/code and preserved outputs.
coding_notes: Level 7; strongest computational provenance.

definition: Relationship/artifact predates analysis/results but data may already exist.
coding_notes: Use for PAPs, protocols, or registered reports when analysis timing is verified.

definition: Relationship/artifact was created after data collection but before results were known or reported.
coding_notes: Use when this distinction is documented.

definition: Relationship/artifact was created after results were known or public.
coding_notes: Do not use as preregistered confirmatory evidence.

definition: Timing is not meaningful for this relationship.
coding_notes: Use for corpus membership, duplicate, and many package-support relationships.

definition: Timing has not been audited or cannot be determined.
coding_notes: Use honestly rather than guessing.

Use for appendices, supplements, and supplemental tables.

definition: Replication intended to repeat the same claim/design closely.

definition: Replication of a construct using different operationalization/design.

Use for computational reproducibility checks.
Use for robustness projects.

definition: Replication plus extension or new conditions.

definition: Pilot/smaller study paired with later full-scale/larger study.

definition: Component study included in pooled round/meta-analysis.
coding_notes: Use for Metaketa and similar pooled designs.

definition: Paper included as a child of a corpus.
coding_notes: Use for membership without result-level numbers.

definition: Result row included as child of a database/corpus.
coding_notes: Use for row-level source assertions.

definition: Relationship inferred only from citation/link metadata.
coding_notes: Use when no value/selector evidence has been copied.
